title: My First Time Cooking Curry
summary: A narrator tries to cook a Thai green curry for the first time, following a recipe step by step. Not everything goes to plan, but the final dish turns out surprisingly well.
body: Last Saturday, I decided to try cooking something new — a Thai green curry. I had never made it before, so I found a recipe online and printed it out. The list of ingredients was quite long, and a few things were difficult to find at my local supermarket. I had to go to a special Asian grocery shop to get the green curry paste and the coconut milk. When I got home, I read through the recipe carefully before I started. The first step was to fry some onion and garlic in oil. That part was easy. Then I added the curry paste and the coconut milk and let them simmer for a few minutes. The kitchen smelled amazing. After that, I put in the chicken and vegetables and turned down the heat. I was supposed to cook it for fifteen minutes, but I got distracted and left it for twenty. I was worried the chicken might be dry, but actually it was perfectly tender. I served it with rice and a squeeze of lime on top. My flatmate tasted it and said it was one of the best things she had ever eaten. I was very surprised — and a little proud. Cooking can be stressful when things go wrong, but it is also very satisfying when they go right.

annotations.exampleEn: She printed out the directions before leaving home.
The recipe only needs five ingredients.
You only need a tablespoon of curry paste for this dish.
Fry the onions in a little oil until they turn golden.

annotations.meaningVi: đun nhỏ lửa, đun liu riu
annotations.exampleEn: Let the sauce simmer for ten minutes to thicken.
Once it starts boiling, turn down the heat.

annotations.meaningVi: bị phân tâm, mất tập trung
annotations.exampleEn: I got distracted by my phone and burned the toast.
The meat was perfectly tender after two hours of cooking.

annotations.meaningVi: một chút nước cốt chanh (vắt chanh)
annotations.exampleEn: Add a squeeze of lime to the fish for extra flavour.

annotations.meaningVi: người ở chung phòng / căn hộ
annotations.exampleEn: My flatmate and I share the cooking on weekdays.
Moving house can be very stressful.

annotations.meaningVi: thỏa mãn, mang lại sự hài lòng
annotations.exampleEn: It is very satisfying to finish a difficult task.

grammarNotes.title: Was supposed to: expressing what was planned
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Was/were supposed to + verb' diễn tả điều gì đó được lên kế hoạch hoặc dự kiến sẽ xảy ra, nhưng thực tế lại khác đi. Ví dụ: 'I was supposed to cook it for fifteen minutes, but I left it for twenty.' — Lẽ ra tôi phải nấu trong 15 phút, nhưng tôi để đến 20 phút.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'was/were supposed to + verb' to talk about what was planned or expected, but didn't happen as intended. Example: 'I was supposed to cook it for fifteen minutes, but I got distracted and left it for twenty.' This structure is common when explaining mistakes or unexpected outcomes.

grammarNotes.title: Past perfect for experiences: 'had never made it before'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì quá khứ hoàn thành (had + past participle) dùng để nói về điều chưa từng xảy ra trước một thời điểm trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: 'I had never made it before' — Trước đây tôi chưa bao giờ nấu món đó. 'It was one of the best things she had ever eaten.' — Đó là một trong những thứ ngon nhất cô ấy từng ăn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past perfect (had + past participle) to describe an experience — or lack of it — before a past moment. Example: 'I had never made it before, so I found a recipe online.' / 'It was one of the best things she had ever eaten.' The key words are 'never', 'ever', 'before', and 'already'.

translationVi: Thứ Bảy tuần trước, tôi quyết định thử nấu một món mới — cà ri xanh Thái Lan. Tôi chưa bao giờ nấu món đó trước đây, vì vậy tôi tìm một công thức trực tuyến và in ra. Danh sách nguyên liệu khá dài, và một số thứ khó tìm tại siêu thị địa phương. Tôi phải đến một cửa hàng thực phẩm Châu Á đặc biệt để mua tương cà ri xanh và nước cốt dừa. Khi về nhà, tôi đọc kỹ công thức trước khi bắt đầu. Bước đầu tiên là xào hành và tỏi với dầu. Phần đó dễ dàng. Sau đó tôi thêm tương cà ri và nước cốt dừa rồi đun liu riu trong vài phút. Căn bếp thơm tuyệt vời. Sau đó, tôi cho gà và rau vào và vặn nhỏ lửa. Lẽ ra tôi phải nấu 15 phút, nhưng tôi bị phân tâm và để đến 20 phút. Tôi lo gà có thể bị khô, nhưng thực ra nó mềm vừa hoàn hảo. Tôi dọn ra với cơm và vắt một chút nước cốt chanh lên trên. Người bạn cùng phòng của tôi nếm thử và nói đó là một trong những thứ ngon nhất cô ấy từng ăn. Tôi rất ngạc nhiên — và hơi tự hào. Nấu ăn có thể căng thẳng khi mọi thứ không theo ý muốn, nhưng cũng rất thỏa mãn khi mọi thứ suôn sẻ.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator decide to cook?
questions.explanation: She decided to cook 'a Thai green curry.'
questions.hint: The dish type and its country of origin are both mentioned.

questions.prompt: Where did she find the recipe?
questions.explanation: She 'found a recipe online and printed it out.'
questions.hint: She used the internet and then made a physical copy.

questions.prompt: Why did she have to go to a special shop?
options: Some ingredients were not available at her local supermarket
She wanted to see the city
questions.explanation: 'A few things were difficult to find at my local supermarket' — she needed to go to an Asian grocery shop.
questions.hint: Some items on the ingredient list were hard to find.

questions.prompt: Which two ingredients did she buy at the Asian grocery shop?
options: Green curry paste and coconut milk
questions.explanation: She bought 'the green curry paste and the coconut milk.'
questions.hint: Both items are essential to this dish.

questions.prompt: What was the first step in the recipe?
options: Frying onion and garlic in oil
questions.explanation: 'The first step was to fry some onion and garlic in oil.'
questions.hint: It involved a frying pan and two common flavouring ingredients.

questions.prompt: What did the kitchen smell like after she added the curry paste and coconut milk?
questions.hint: One word is used to describe the smell.

questions.prompt: How long was she supposed to cook the chicken for?
questions.explanation: 'I was supposed to cook it for fifteen minutes.'
questions.hint: This is the planned time, not the actual time.

questions.prompt: Why did she cook the chicken for longer than planned?
options: She wanted it to be extra tender
questions.explanation: 'I got distracted and left it for twenty' minutes.

questions.prompt: How did the chicken turn out?
questions.hint: Despite the extra cooking time, it was a positive result.

questions.prompt: What did she serve the curry with?
options: Rice and a squeeze of lime
questions.explanation: She 'served it with rice and a squeeze of lime on top.'
questions.hint: Two accompaniments are mentioned — a starchy side and a citrus fruit.

questions.prompt: What did her flatmate say about the curry?
options: It was too spicy for her
It was good but not her favourite
It was one of the best things she had ever eaten
questions.explanation: Her flatmate 'said it was one of the best things she had ever eaten.'
questions.hint: Her flatmate's reaction was very positive.

cloze.template: Last Saturday, I decided to try cooking something new — a Thai green {{b1}}. I had never made it before, so I found a recipe online and {{b2}} it out. The list of {{b3}} was quite long, and a few things were difficult to find at my local supermarket. I had to go to a special Asian grocery shop to get the green curry paste and the coconut milk. When I got home, I read through the recipe carefully before I started. The first step was to {{b4}} some onion and garlic in oil. That part was easy. Then I added the curry paste and the coconut milk and let them {{b5}} for a few minutes. The kitchen smelled amazing. After that, I put in the chicken and vegetables and turned down the {{b6}}. I was supposed to cook it for fifteen minutes, but I got {{b7}} and left it for twenty. I was worried the chicken might be dry, but actually it was perfectly {{b8}}. I served it with rice and a squeeze of {{b9}} on top. My {{b10}} tasted it and said it was one of the best things she had ever eaten. I was very surprised — and a little proud. Cooking can be {{b11}} when things go wrong, but it is also very satisfying when they go right.

blanks.explanation: 'curry' — the dish she cooked was a Thai green curry.
'printed' — she printed out the recipe so she could follow it in the kitchen.
'ingredients' — the list of things needed to cook a recipe.
'fry' — to cook food in hot oil in a pan.
'simmer' — to cook gently just below boiling point.
'heat' — 'turned down the heat' means reduced the cooking temperature.
'distracted' — she lost focus and left the chicken cooking longer than planned.
'tender' — perfectly tender means soft, juicy, and easy to eat.
'lime' — a squeeze of lime adds freshness and acidity to a curry.
'flatmate' — a person who shares a flat or apartment with you.
'stressful' — cooking can be stressful when things go wrong, but satisfying when they go right.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator worried when things didn't go perfectly, but the curry was still delicious. Do you think being a good cook requires following recipes exactly, or is it better to trust your own instincts and be willing to experiment? What is the best approach to learning a new skill?
